pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh line 46 and export_result.sh line 11 - file is created and analyzed by task scripts
Verified in setup_task.sh line 76 and export_result.sh line 12 - file is the expected export target

ISA P&ID standard naming convention; PSV = Pressure Safety Valve. Verified in verifier.py line 71-76 and setup_task.sh hazop_recommendations.txt - follows real engineering notation where P=Pressure, SV=Safety Valve, 101=tag number. Web search confirmed PSV is standard abbreviation for Pressure Safety Valves per https://enggcyclopedia.com/2011/04/pressure-safety-valves-typical-pid-arrangement/

ISA P&ID standard naming convention; P = Pump, 101 = tag number. Verified in verifier.py line 71-76 and setup_task.sh - standard pump identification notation. Confirmed by multiple P&ID references per https://www.projectmaterials.com/epc-projects/engineering/pid-symbols-list/

ISA P&ID standard naming convention; V = Valve (check valve), 102 = tag number. Verified in verifier.py line 71-76 and setup_task.sh - check valves are standard P&ID components documented at https://enggcyclopedia.com/2011/01/valve-pid-symbols/

ISA P&ID standard naming convention; E = Exchanger (shell and tube heat exchanger), 101 = tag number. Verified in verifier.py line 71-76 and setup_task.sh - standard heat exchanger identification. Confirmed per https://www.projectmaterials.com/epc-projects/engineering/pid-symbols-list/ where E = Equipment/Exchanger

Verified in verifier.py line 92 where 'valve' is one of six pid_keywords checked: ["pid", "valve", "pump", "exchanger", "vessel", "mxgraph.pid"]. Draw.io has dedicated valves.xml shape library per https://github.com/vmassol/draw.io/blob/master/war/stencils/pid/valves.xml

Verified in verifier.py line 92 where 'pump' is one of six pid_keywords checked. Draw.io has dedicated pumps.xml shape library per https://github.com/vmassol/draw.io/blob/master/war/stencils/pid/pumps.xml with centrifugal pump and other pump types

Partial verification: 'heat' is NOT in the verifier's pid_keywords list (line 92). Verifier checks for 'exchanger' instead. However, 'heat' is reasonable shorthand for 'heat exchanger' components. Draw.io has heat_exchangers.xml per https://github.com/vmassol/draw.io/blob/master/war/stencils/pid/heat_exchangers.xml. The 'exchanger' keyword in verifier would catch 'heat_exchanger' shape names.
Verified in verifier.py line 92 where 'exchanger' is one of six pid_keywords checked. Heat exchangers are documented P&ID symbols per https://enggcyclopedia.com/2011/02/heat-exchanger-pid-symbols/

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task where the diagram and all data are created by the setup script. The four required equipment tags (PSV-101, P-101, V-102, E-101) follow real ISA P&ID (Piping & Instrumentation Diagram) standard naming conventions where each tag combines equipment type letters (P=Pump, E=Exchanger, V=Valve, PSV=Pressure Safety Valve) with numeric identifiers. All four components are real engineering equipment with documented P&ID symbols available in draw.io/diagrams.net via the mxgraph.pid library. The 'required_styles' metadata lists expected shape keywords, though the verifier code (line 92) actually checks for six keywords: 'pid', 'valve', 'pump', 'exchanger', 'vessel', 'mxgraph.pid' - three of which match the metadata but 'heat' does not appear in the verifier's keyword list despite being a shorthand for heat exchangers.
